meta.title: Checkout — promo code handling
meta.task: Understand product scope before fixing promo-code rounding bug
meta.analysis_scope: Cart + checkout promo flow as the shopper experiences it

nodes.label: Shopper
nodes.summary: Buys products; applies promo codes at checkout.

nodes.label: Promo codes
nodes.summary: Percentage and fixed-amount discounts applied to the cart total.

nodes.label: One promo per order
nodes.summary: Business rule: promos do not stack.

nodes.label: Loyalty points interaction?
nodes.evidence: loyalty service reads cart_total in loyalty/accrual.ts:15 — discount may change points earned

flows.title: Apply promo code at checkout
flows.goal: Pay less using a promo code

steps.explanation: Shopper enters code SAVE10 in the cart.
steps.business_why: Discounts drive conversion; entry point is the cart.

steps.explanation: Cart asks promo capability to validate and price the discount; single-promo rule enforced.
Shopper proceeds to checkout; discounted total shown.
steps.business_why: Total transparency before payment reduces abandonment.

advisory.summary: Promo application is cart-centric; checkout only displays results. Loyalty interaction is the open risk.

notes.note: Rounding of percentage discounts is where the reported bug lives.

open_questions: BB1: does discount change loyalty accrual?
advisory.residual_uncertainty: Marketing attribution of promo usage not scanned.
